

# reto
# Descripción
Class, take your seats! It's PRIME-time for a quiz...
Additional details will be available after launching your challenge instance.

# Solución 
## solución
```
Paso 1 - Tenemos p y q por lo tanto podemos calcular n  ?   Y
Problema
Inputs and outputs are in decimal. No hex here!
#### NEW PROBLEM ####
q : 60413
p : 76753
##### PRODUCE THE FOLLOWING ####
n
IS THIS POSSIBLE and FEASIBLE? (Y/N):y
#### TIME TO SHOW ME WHAT YOU GOT! ###
n: 4636878989
Outstanding move!!!

Cálculo
>>> p = 60413
>>> q = 76753
>>> n  = p * q
>>> n
4636878989
 
Paso 2 - Tenemos p y n , podemos calcular q ? Y
Problema
#### NEW PROBLEM ####
p : 54269
n : 5051846941                                                          
##### PRODUCE THE FOLLOWING ####                                        
q                                                                       
IS THIS POSSIBLE and FEASIBLE? (Y/N):y                                  
#### TIME TO SHOW ME WHAT YOU GOT! ###                                  
q: 93089                                                                
Outstanding move!!!

Cálculo
>>> p = 54269
>>> n = 5051846941
>>> q = n // p
>>> q
93089
Paso 3 - Tenemos e y n, podemos calcular p y q ? N, n es muy grande para factorizar
Problema
#### NEW PROBLEM ####
e : 3
n : 
##### PRODUCE THE FOLLOWING ####
q
p
IS THIS POSSIBLE and FEASIBLE? (Y/N):n
Outstanding move!!!
Paso 4 - Tenemos p y q, podemos calcular totien(n) ? Y
Problema
#### NEW PROBLEM ####
q : 66347
p : 12611
##### PRODUCE THE FOLLOWING ####
totient(n)
IS THIS POSSIBLE and FEASIBLE? (Y/N):y
#### TIME TO SHOW ME WHAT YOU GOT! ###
totient(n): 836623060
Outstanding move!!!

Cálculo
>>> p = 12611
>>> q = 66347
>>> tn = (p -1) * (q-1)
>>> tn
836623060
Paso 5 - Tenemos m (plaintext), e, n, podemos calcular c (cipertext) ? Y
Problema
#### NEW PROBLEM ####
plaintext : 6357294171489311547190987615544575133581967886499484091352661406414044440475205342882841236357665973431462491355089413710392273380203038793241564304774271529108729717
e : 3
n : 
##### PRODUCE THE FOLLOWING ####
ciphertext
IS THIS POSSIBLE and FEASIBLE? (Y/N):y
#### TIME TO SHOW ME WHAT YOU GOT! ###
ciphertext: 
Outstanding move!!!
Cálculo
>>> m = 6357294171489311547190987615544575133581967886499484091352661406414044440475205342882841236357665973431462491355089413710392273380203038793241564304774271529108729717
>>> e = 3
>>> n = 
>>> c = pow(m, e, n)
>>> c

Paso 6 - Tenemos c (cipertext), e, n, podemos calcular m (plaintext) ? N , n muy grande para factorizar
Problema
#### NEW PROBLEM ####
ciphertext : 
e : 3
n : 
##### PRODUCE THE FOLLOWING ####
plaintext
IS THIS POSSIBLE and FEASIBLE? (Y/N):n
Paso 7
Problema
#### NEW PROBLEM ####
p : 
ciphertext : 
e : 65537
n : 
##### PRODUCE THE FOLLOWING ####
plaintext
IS THIS POSSIBLE and FEASIBLE? (Y/N): Y
Cálculo
 >>> p = 
>>> n = 
>>> e = 65537
>>> c = 
>>> 
>>> q = n // p
>>> tn = (p -1) * (q-1)
>>> d = pow(e, -1, tn)
>>> m = pow(c, d, n)
>>> m
218378661235194013475375491560393839271890611748313869466505982182430952502727152060029
>>> flag = bytes.fromhex(hex(m)[2:]).decode()
>>> flag
>>> 
```

## solución 2




# referencias
```
RSA - llave publica - asimetrico
-------------------------------------------------------------------------
m   - mensaje original o mensaje en texto plano 
c   - mensaje cifrado (ciphertext)
p,q - son dos numeros primos distintos y muy grandes
n   - es el modulo (lo compartes las llaves publica como privada)
tn  - totient n (funcion de euler)
e   - llave public - 65537 (exponente)  2 ^ 16 + 1
d   - llave privada

Calculos
--------------------------------------------------
n  = p * q
tn = (p -1) * (q-1)
d = e ^ -1 (mod tn)	- pow(e, -1, tn)

Cifrar  
--------------------------------------------------
c = m ^ e (mod n)	- pow(m, e, n)

Decifrars
--------------------------------------------------
m = c ^ d (mod n)	- pow(c, d, n)

```
